title: Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf

seo.title: How to Make Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf — Easy Japanese Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf. This easy Japanese recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.
longTailKeywords: how to make Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf
easy Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf recipe
authentic Japanese Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf

seo.h1: Authentic Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf Recipe
h2Headings: What is Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf?

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Japanese Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf — a beloved Japanese classic

description: Shokupan, the quintessential Japanese milk bread, is renowned for its incredibly soft, fluffy texture and delicate sweetness. This loaf uses the Tangzhong method to ensure an exceptionally moist crumb that stays fresh for days. Enjoy it toasted with butter, as a sandwich base, or simply on its own.

instructions.title: Prepare the Tangzhong
instructions.text: In a small saucepan, whisk together 15g bread flour and 15g water until smooth. Cook over medium-low heat, stirring constantly, until the mixture thickens to a paste-like consistency, about 2-3 minutes. It should form ripples as you stir. Remove from heat, transfer to a small bowl, cover with plastic wrap directly on the surface, and let it cool to room temperature.

instructions.title: Mix the Dough
instructions.text: In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooled Tangzhong, 250g bread flour, 170ml lukewarm milk, 30g granulated sugar, 5g instant dry yeast, and 3g salt. Mix with a spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface.

instructions.title: Knead the Dough
instructions.text: Knead the dough for about 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic. It will be sticky at first, but keep kneading. Incorporate the 25g softened unsalted butter and continue kneading for another 5-7 minutes until the butter is fully absorbed and the dough passes the windowpane test (a small piece can be stretched thinly without tearing).

instructions.title: First Proof
instructions.text: Form the dough into a ball and place it in a lightly oiled bowl. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until it has doubled in size. This is the first proofing.

instructions.title: Shape the Loaf
instructions.text: Gently deflate the dough and divide it into three equal pieces. Roll each piece into a flat oval, then fold the longer sides towards the center. Roll it up tightly from one end to form a cylinder. Place the three cylinders seam-side down into a greased 9x5 inch loaf pan, ensuring they are snug.

instructions.title: Second Proof and Bake
instructions.text: Cover the loaf pan with plastic wrap or a damp cloth and let it rise again in a warm place for 45-60 minutes, or until the dough has risen about 1 inch above the rim of the pan. Preheat your oven to 180°C (350°F). Optionally, brush the top with an egg wash for a golden crust. Bake for 30-35 minutes, or until golden brown and cooked through. If the top browns too quickly, loosely tent with foil.

instructions.title: Cool and Serve
instructions.text: Once baked, immediately remove the loaf from the pan and place it on a wire rack to cool completely. Slicing the bread while it is still warm may result in a gummy texture. Once fully cooled, slice and enjoy your incredibly soft Shokupan.

tips: The Tangzhong is crucial for Shokupan's signature softness and moisture retention. Ensure it is cooled completely before adding to the main dough.
Achieving the 'windowpane test' during kneading is key. It indicates proper gluten development, which is essential for a fluffy, airy texture. Do not rush this step.
Proper proofing is vital. For the second proof, ensure the dough rises sufficiently but not excessively, or it may collapse during baking. A warm, humid environment helps.
article: Shokupan Japanese Milk Bread Loaf is a beloved Japanese Baking dish enjoyed across Japanese. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
